Title: BolAG-BolRPI2-BolBAM4 Module Regulates the Bolting of Curd by Modulating Starch Metabolism in Broccoli (Brassica oleracea var. italica)

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: energy supply and signaling associated with reproductive development and bolting
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: hydrolyzes alpha-1,4-glucosidic bonds in starch to release maltose during starch degradation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes starch degradation and contributes to soluble sugar accumulation associated with premature bolting
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BolBAM4 was significantly upregulated in BolAG-overexpressing broccoli. 2. BolAG directly bound the BolBAM4 promoter and activated its transcription. 3. Co-expression of BolAG and BolRPI2 further enhanced BolBAM4 promoter activity, linking BolBAM4 to accelerated starch degradation and soluble sugar accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; qRT-PCR; yeast one-hybrid; dual-luciferase reporter assay
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: highly expressed in curds, flowers and seeds
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: highly expressed in curds, flowers and seeds; induced in BolAG-overexpressing plants
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: BolBAM4 native promoter (~2000 bp) in reporter assays; CaMV 35S promoter for effector constructs
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 16 h light/8 h dark; dark-to-light transitions used to assess starch turnover
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: enhanced starch degradation is associated with premature bolting, reducing curd marketability
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate downstream target for tuning starch remobilization and delaying premature bolting in broccoli; should be manipulated with caution to avoid excess soluble sugar-driven early bolting
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although central to the module, direct transgenic manipulation of BolBAM4 itself was not reported in this paper.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports chloroplast function, carbohydrate accumulation, and energy supply for curd development/bolting
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: leaves; curd inferred for developmental relevance
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: pentose phosphate pathway linked to starch metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: catalyzes the reversible interconversion between ribose-5-phosphate and ribulose-5-phosphate
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes chloroplast development, starch synthesis and soluble sugar accumulation, thereby accelerating curd development and bolting-related processes
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BolRPI2 interacts physically with BolAG and is promoted to nuclear localization by BolAG. 2. Overexpression of BolRPI2 increased chlorophyll content, chloroplast size/number, starch accumulation and soluble sugar content. 3. BolRPI2 enhanced BolAG-mediated activation of BolBAM4, linking pentose phosphate metabolism to starch degradation control.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: overexpression and RNAi lines; chlorophyll measurement; TEM; metabolite profiling; yeast two-hybrid; BiFC; subcellular localization; dual-luciferase reporter assay
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: transcription repressed by BolAG overexpression and increased in RNAi-BolAG lines
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: cytoplasm and nucleus; nuclear colocalization with BolAG
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter for overexpression and effector constructs; BolRPI2 native promoter (~2000 bp) in reporter assays
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 16 h light/8 h dark; in vitro etiolation/dark treatment for chlorophyll and starch analyses
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: overexpression is associated with precocious curd formation/premature bolting, which may reduce marketability
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful target to modulate chloroplast performance and carbohydrate status, but deployment should avoid inducing premature bolting
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Despite being an enzyme gene, the paper also establishes a noncanonical co-regulatory role through physical interaction with the TF BolAG that boosts BolBAM4 transcription.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: energy supply and signaling associated with curd development and bolting
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes starch synthesis and starch degradation, increases soluble sugar accumulation, and accelerates curd development/premature bolting
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BolAG is highly expressed in curd and floral organs and is induced by sucrose. 2. Overexpression causes premature bolting, whereas RNAi delays or abolishes bolting. 3. BolAG directly activates BolBAM4 and represses BolRPI2 transcription, while also interacting with BolRPI2 to further enhance BolBAM4 expression. 4. BolAG upregulates starch synthesis genes BolGBSS1 and BolAPL3, promoting starch synthesis independently of BolRPI2.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transgenic overexpression and RNAi in broccoli; qRT-PCR; starch and soluble sugar assays; RNA-seq; Y1H; dual-luciferase reporter assay; Y2H; BiFC; subcellular localization
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation of BolBAM4 and starch synthesis genes; transcriptional repression of BolRPI2; protein interaction-mediated enhancement of target activation
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: starch biosynthesis; starch degradation; soluble sugar accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates BolBAM4; represses BolRPI2; upregulates BolGBSS1 and BolAPL3
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: major developmental-metabolic switch linking carbohydrate metabolism to curd bolting
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: sucrose induces BolAG expression, suggesting sugar-responsive feedforward regulation
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: exogenous sucrose; developmental state of curd/floral organs
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: forms a functional protein interaction module with BolRPI2 to enhance BolBAM4 transcription
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: predominantly expressed in curd and flower
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: predominantly expressed in curd and flower; higher in early-bolting cultivars; induced by 1% sucrose
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter for overexpression/effector constructs; BolBAM4 and BolRPI2 native promoters in reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 1% sucrose treatment; 16 h light/8 h dark; field transplantation for bolting phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: premature bolting leads to small/loose curds and reduced market value
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: high-value target for breeding against premature bolting by downregulation/editing or promoter tuning; overexpression is undesirable for marketable curd production
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Gene ID 106305460; homolog of Arabidopsis AGAMOUS; MADS-box domain required for interaction with BolRPI2 and binding to CArG-containing promoters.
